title: The Paradox of "Newness" in Personal Branding
desc:
In an age where templates and AI-generated content are everywhere, many people use different styles in their posts
However, long exposure to these different styles can make us feel "numb" to design
content:
# The Paradox of "Newness" in Personal Branding

In an age where templates and AI-generated content are everywhere, many people use different styles in their posts. However, long exposure to these different styles can make us feel "numb" to design.

## The Paradox of Choice
New templates and AI-generated styles surface every day. This creates a paradox: what we call "new" doesn't actually feel "new" anymore because we see it every day.

![Alt: A circular arrow diagram showing the repetitive cycle of finding and using new styles, representing the design paradox]

---

## A Different Approach: Consistency Over Novelty
Most people don't actually recognize the specific templates you use; they only care about how the content is presented. In fact, it takes more mental energy for the brain to process a brand-new layout every time.

So, I asked myself: **What if I stand out by reusing the same template over and over again?** Would it feel "newer" than using a new template every day?

![Alt: A lightbulb icon representing the strategic idea of using consistency as a tool to stand out](/images/articles/the-branding-paradox/idea.png)

---

### The Result of One Year of Consistency
I have been reusing this exact carousel for over a year—in college, at work, and even for informal discussions. The funny thing is, people now easily recognize me by my **color pattern** alone, yet they rarely remember a random template they only see once.

![Alt: A fingerprint icon symbolizing a unique and recognizable personal identity through consistent design](/images/articles/the-branding-paradox/template.png)

---

## Unifying My Digital Presence
Even though people like new things, they often crave something consistent. With that in mind, I decided to apply my signature color pattern across my entire digital footprint, from LinkedIn and GitHub to my personal website.

### 1. LinkedIn & GitHub
I aligned my professional profiles to ensure a seamless transition for anyone looking at my work.

![Alt: Screenshot of Muhammad Zaini's LinkedIn profile showing the consistent dark theme and yellow accent colors](/images/articles/the-branding-paradox/linkedin.png)
![Alt: Screenshot of Muhammad Zaini's GitHub profile showing the same visual identity and "ζ" logo](/images/articles/the-branding-paradox/github.png)

### 2. The Personal Portfolio
The final destination is my website, where the branding comes together in its full form.

![Alt: Screenshot of Zaini's Portfolio Website showcasing the unified 'Game Developer' branding and project gallery](/images/articles/the-branding-paradox/website.png)

---

**How do you improve your branding?** Do you prefer constant evolution or doubling down on consistency? Tell me your thoughts!
